bull_thesis: ASML is the single most load-bearing company in the AI buildout: a literal monopoly on EUV lithography, the non-bypassable chokepoint through which every <7nm chip on earth must pass. The dossier traces management's narrative hardening from defensively-hedged ('we cannot confirm 2026 growth,' Q2'25) to a supply-constrained boom ('memory customers are sold out for 2026 and supply constraint will last beyond 2026,' Q1'26), and the financials corroborate it: FY25 revenue EUR32.67B (+15.6%), net income EUR9.61B (+27%), EPS EUR24.71 (+28.4%), with TTM EPS now EUR25.87. The moat lie-detector reads positive: gross margin held/expanded at 52.8% (quarterly prints 51.6-54%) on rising revenue = pricing power, not commoditization. Returns are software-like for capital equipment (ROE 54.1%, ROIC 30.6%, FCF margin 32.6%), the balance sheet is net CASH EUR5.67B, and capital allocation is exemplary (large buybacks into strength, growing dividend, strategic 11% Mistral stake). The theme is verifiably durable and multi-year: backlog EUR38.8B extending into 2027, 2026 guidance raised and narrowed to EUR36-40B, EUV Low-NA output targeted >=60 units (2026) rising to >=80 (2027), SK Hynix placing a record ~$8B EUV order, and ~$400B of 2026 hyperscaler AI capex flowing downstream into TSMC/Samsung/SK Hynix/Intel/Micron fabs. NVIDIA wafer-intensity (Blackwell 2.5 -> Rubin 10 wafers by 2027 = 4x) drives volume on top of rising EUV layer-count (litho intensity) — a double tailwind. High-NA (EXE) is a fresh S-curve with design wins across all five leading-edge buyers and 3-to-1 mask reduction; advanced packaging (XT260) opens an adjacent TAM lane. Reinvestment economics are elite: incremental ROIC well above cost of capital with a 2030 CMD target of EUR44-60B revenue at 56-60% GM. CONDITIONS THAT MUST HOLD: AI capex stays a multi-year supercycle (not a one-year air-pocket); China/MATCH-Act DUV restrictions stay a bounded single-digit-% drag; High-NA reaches HVM maturity to deliver the 2030 margin step-up. If they hold, you own the irreplaceable toll-collector of the most important technology buildout of the decade.
bear_thesis: The bear case is almost entirely a PRICE case, not a quality case — and that is precisely why it bites. At EUR1,863 ASML trades at 63x FCF and ~35% ABOVE every intrinsic estimate (Buffett FV EUR1,121, DCF FV EUR542), sits +50% above its 200-day SMA at 97% of its 52-week range, and trades BELOW the EUR1,694 analyst consensus target (-9% implied). Every valuation model agrees the stock discounts the 2030 aspiration (EUR44-60B / 56-60% GM), not the actual high-teens-growth trajectory. The fundamental risks that could trigger a derate: (1) AI capex is a CAPEX cycle, and capital equipment is the most violently cyclical link in it — a single year of hyperscaler digestion turns a +15% grower into a flat-to-down one, and at 63x FCF the multiple compression alone is brutal (a derate to a still-premium 30x TTM EPS = ~EUR776). The 'sold out for 2026' visibility does NOT extend to 2027+. (2) China is a live, escalating risk: a EUR1.4B de-booking is ALREADY realized (Q2'25), China was >25% of 2025 revenue moderating to ~20% in 2026, and the proposed MATCH Act would cut DUV machine sales AND the high-margin service stream to China — a single-digit-% revenue hit that lands disproportionately on margin. (3) The litho-intensity 'pause' the bears flagged (no EUV-layer growth at 2nm until gate-all-around / ~1.4nm) — management conceded 'one node... a bit of a pause' (Q2'25). (4) High-NA is openly margin-DILUTIVE today and still in phase I/II (maturity, not HVM) — the 2030 56-60% GM thesis is unbanked. (5) Severe customer concentration (a handful of leading-edge buyers; TSMC the largest) and the 1,700-job reorg at a cyclical peak. WHAT CONFIRMS THE BEAR: any 2027 capex-digestion signal from TSMC/memory, a hard MATCH-Act enactment, or a single EUV-bookings miss — into a 63x multiple with the stock at the top of its range and consensus already below spot.
sop_bull: Favorable segment Sum-of-Parts, anchored on earnings power (ASML's segments share one factory/margin structure, so a blended earnings-power SoP is more robust than EV/Sales-by-part, which is shown for completeness). EARNINGS-POWER ANCHOR: TTM EPS EUR25.87 x a monopoly-justified 46-50x (vs ~35x sector-premium baseline, paying up for the durable AI-supercycle + High-NA optionality) = EUR1,190-1,294/sh, + net cash EUR14.6/sh ~ EUR1,205-1,309/sh. SEGMENT EV/SALES VIEW (FY25): NXE EUV Low-NA EUR10.45B x14 = EUR146B; ArFi immersion EUR10.31B x8 = EUR82B; EXE High-NA EUR1.16B x16 = EUR19B; KrF EUR1.0B x6 = EUR6B; Metrology/inspection EUR0.825B x10 = EUR8B; ArF Dry EUR0.43B x5 = EUR2B; I-Line EUR0.31B x4 = EUR1B; Installed Base/service EUR8.19B x11 (SaaS-like recurring) = EUR90B. Sum EV ~EUR354B + net cash EUR5.67B = ~EUR360B / 388.9M sh ~ EUR925/sh (the sales-multiple view is conservative on ASML's 30%+ FCF margins and understates the earnings anchor). BULL SoP per share: EUR1,250 (earnings-power-weighted).
sop_bear: Adverse Sum-of-Parts ASSUMING THE THEME PAUSES 12-18 MONTHS (one year of hyperscaler/memory capex digestion + a harder China/MATCH-Act bite). EARNINGS-POWER ANCHOR: normalize TTM EPS EUR25.87 down ~5% for a flat/soft year to ~EUR24.6, apply a derated 25-30x (still a premium to cyclical semicap, reflecting the intact monopoly) = EUR615-738/sh, + net cash EUR14.6 ~ EUR630-753/sh. SEGMENT EV/SALES VIEW (volume-haircut + multiple derate): NXE x0.80 vol x8 = EUR67B; ArFi x0.75 x5 = EUR39B (China/DUV most exposed); EXE x0.70 x7 = EUR6B; KrF x0.85 x4 = EUR3B; Metro x0.85 x6 = EUR4B; ArF Dry x0.85 x3 = EUR1B; I-Line x0.90 x3 = EUR1B; Installed Base/service EUR8.19B x8 = EUR66B. Sum EV ~EUR187B + net cash EUR5.67B = ~EUR192B / 388.9M sh ~ EUR494/sh. BEAR SoP per share: EUR620 (blended toward the earnings anchor; the EUR494 sales-view is the deep-derate tail). Note: even the bear case is a healthy, profitable, net-cash monopoly — the downside is multiple compression on a great business, not impairment.
